qa_pairs.question: What are the page limit requirements for applications submitted to the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC)?
qa_pairs.answer: Applications for the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) must not exceed 10 pages before the References section. If the total application, including References, is 10 pages or less, all documents can be submitted together; otherwise, References must be submitted as a separate document. All application documents are required to be submitted in PDF format, using the latest version of the provided template.

qa_pairs.question: Under what circumstances would a CPU request for the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) be rejected?
qa_pairs.answer: A CPU request for the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) will be rejected if the total core-years are split across multiple clusters (e.g., 100 cores on Fir and 150 cores on Nibi) such that the request for each individual cluster falls below the minimum eligibility of 200 core-years. Such requests are typically rejected because they may be able to be met through the Rapid Access Service (RAS) without needing a RAC allocation.

qa_pairs.question: When can an applicant include resource requests below the minimum eligible amounts in their Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application?
qa_pairs.answer: Applicants can include requests for resources that are individually below the minimum eligible amounts under two conditions: (1) if at least one other resource requested in the same application, or for the same cloud site, meets or exceeds its respective minimum (e.g., requesting 50 TB of project storage and 50 TB of nearline storage on Fir, where only the project storage meets the minimum), or (2) if requesting cloud resources, as long as one of the resources for a particular cloud site meets the minimum, all other cloud resources for that site must be included, even if they are below their individual minimums (e.g., 100 VCPU of compute cloud resources on Arbutus, alongside 5 TB of Volume and snapshot storage and 5 TB of object storage).
